prompt: The override does not work — the parent version always runs. What is the bug?
choices: Sub must use the @Override annotation for methods to override parent methods
Sub.greet has a different signature (adds a second parameter) so it is an overload, not an override; Base.greet(String) is never replaced
static classes cannot override methods from their enclosing class
Polymorphism only applies to constructors, not regular methods
explanation: Override requires an identical signature. Sub.greet(String, String) is a new overload, not an override of Base.greet(String). b.greet("Alice") matches Base.greet(String) and no override exists. Adding @Override to Sub's method would show the compile error. Fix: Sub.greet must take exactly (String name) to override.
